compiled_state.title: validate.sh: check_hook_drift() rc=skip falls through to FAIL in report_check()/tally_check()
compiled_state.author: Test

comments.body: Bug details: validate.sh has a check_hook_drift() function that correctly detects when .pre-commit-config.yaml is absent and returns rc=skip. However, report_check() and tally_check() do not handle the 'skip' return value — they fall through to the FAIL branch, reporting a false failure. File: validate.sh in DSO plugin. Functions: check_hook_drift(), report_check(), tally_check(). Trigger: .pre-commit-config.yaml is absent from the host project (e.g., host projects that do not use pre-commit framework). Expected behavior: check is reported as SKIP (yellow, not counted in FAIL total). Actual behavior: check is reported as FAIL. Plugin version when reported: DSO v1.11.10. Fix location: validate.sh in this repo (plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh or equivalent). The skip return code path must be handled as a non-failure in both report_check() and tally_check().
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation complete: This bug was already fixed in commit 93c5e531 ('fix(d494-31b7): validate.sh skip handler'). Both report_check() (line 936) and tally_check() (line 1004) now have 'elif [ "$rc" = "skip" ]' branches that correctly handle the skip case without reporting FAIL. No code change needed.
comments.author: Test
